Supported flows include Authorization Code Grant (for user-level data) and Client Credentials Grant (for app-level data like ads). Refresh tokens are also used for long-term access.\\n  - URL: https://developers.pinterest.com/docs/getting-started/set-up-authentication-and-authorization/\\n\\n- **Access/Gating & Approval Requirements**: Pinterest uses 'Access Tiers'. \\n  - **Trial Access**: Initial tier for development. Requires app creation and review. Includes 'Sandbox' entities visible only to the developer.\\n  - **Standard Access**: Higher limits for production. Requires a further upgrade request and approval.\\n  - URL: https://developers.pinterest.com/docs/getting-started/connect-app/\\n  - Access Tiers Guide: https://developers.pinterest.com/docs/getting-started/access-tiers/\\n\\n- **P...[truncated]

raw_result.description: Pinterest offers a developer platform for building apps against its v5 REST API for organic content, analytics, and ads use cases.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs describe OAuth 2.0 with Authorization Code Grant and Client Credentials Grant; refresh tokens are also documented for ongoing access.
raw_result.gating_reason: API access is organized into Trial and Standard access tiers, and official docs indicate app review/approval is required for Trial access and an upgrade request/approval is required for Standard access.

api_surface.breadth: Public v5 REST API with docs for core Pinterest platform and ads-related use cases, plus rate limits, SDK docs, and reference pages.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Pinterest MCP server or MCP documentation was found in the reviewed first-party sources; official developer tools page points to a Python SDK instead.

raw_result.blocker: Public docs exist, but production-usable API access is gated behind Pinterest app review/access-tier approval rather than clearly self-serve activation.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official rate-limit page indicates Trial tier is limited to 1,000 calls per day, while Standard tier allows 100 calls per second per user per app.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official Python SDK is available on GitHub. Trial access uses sandbox entities visible only to the developer. First-party docs do not surface explicit API pricing, suggesting access is controlled mainly by approval tiers rather than transparent self-serve billing.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The official pages clearly indicate approval-gated access tiers, but first-party evidence reviewed here does not fully clarify whether any developer can self-serve into Trial immediately before review; pricing/monetization details are also not explicitly stated.
